steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says there can be significant within-species variations — changes in population size for a single species over time. The example must show one species fluctuating notably across the months shown in the graph.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'striated rockskipper population was at its highest in January and April of 2001 and at its lowest in July of 2001': This names a within-species shift, but the graph does not show rockskipper reaching its peak in January — this misreads the data. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Indo-Pacific sergeant population ranged from a low of 2 individuals in January of 2001 to a high of 28 individuals in October of 2001': This accurately reads the graph and shows a dramatic swing within one species across time — exactly what "within-species variation" means. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Cocos frillgoby population reached its highest level in April of 2001, when 10 individuals were observed': Citing only the peak gives no sense of variation over time. ✗
  • Choice D — 'striated rockskipper population never exceeded 5 individuals, whereas the Indo-Pacific sergeant population reached 28 individuals in October of 2001': The word "whereas" contrasts two different species — that illustrates between-species differences, not within-species variation. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
